candidates.name: youtube-transcript-api as T27 primary extraction layer
candidates.detail: pip install youtube-transcript-api. One-line caption fetch with language selection, translation, SRT/WebVTT output. 7K stars, MIT, 100% coverage, 2 deps. First adoption recommendation in analysis series.

candidates.name: Innertube API reverse-engineering approach
candidates.detail: YouTube captions fetched via undocumented /youtubei/v1/player API with ANDROID client context. No API key needed. Fragile (YouTube can change), but library maintains compatibility across 8 years.

candidates.name: Context-aware error hierarchy pattern
candidates.detail: 15+ specific exception types with CAUSE_MESSAGE templates. RequestBlocked.cause adapts message based on proxy config type. Each error explains why it happened AND what to do. Gold standard for content extraction error handling.

candidates.name: IP ban workaround operational guide
candidates.detail: Production-tested guide to handling YouTube IP blocks: generic proxies, Webshare rotating residential IPs, retry strategies. The operational knowledge T27 needs.

candidates.name: Three-layer T27 extraction architecture
candidates.detail: Cross-repo synthesis insight: Layer 1 = youtube-transcript-api (instant, free, ~80%). Layer 2 = pytubefix captions (backup). Layer 3 = faster-whisper (local GPU, uncaptioned only). First time 3 analyzed repos compose into a clear architecture.

candidates.name: Proxy rotation infrastructure (Webshare)
candidates.detail: ProxyConfig ABC -> GenericProxyConfig, WebshareProxyConfig. Rotating residential IPs, retry-on-429, connection-close for forced rotation. Production proxy pattern.

candidates.name: SRT/WebVTT timestamp formatting
candidates.detail: Clean timestamp conversion with SRT (comma separator) vs WebVTT (dot separator) formatting. Proper subtitle file generation.

candidates.name: Manual vs auto-generated transcript priority
candidates.detail: find_transcript() checks manually_created first, then generated. Manual = human-verified quality. Auto-generated may have errors. Quality hierarchy baked into architecture.

candidates.name: 100% coverage + 2-dep library packaging
candidates.detail: Poetry with lock file, py.typed marker, --fail-under=100, only requests + defusedxml runtime deps. Reference for well-packaged Python library.

candidates.name: Don't build your own transcript fetcher (anti-pattern)
candidates.detail: 8 years and 399 commits maintaining compatibility with YouTube's changing API. Cookie auth broke, consent flow changed, innertube API evolved. Use the library, don't replicate the maintenance burden.

candidates.name: Ignore IP blocking for bulk extraction (anti-pattern)
candidates.detail: YouTube blocks bulk requests. The extensive proxy infrastructure exists because this is the #1 operational challenge. Plan for it from the start.

cross_repo_connections.detail: This repo is Layer 1 (captions, instant). Bulk-transcribe is Layer 3 (Whisper, GPU fallback). Together they cover captioned AND uncaptioned videos.
This repo is local/free caption extraction. Bedrock is AWS-managed transcription. Different cost/complexity tradeoffs for T27 architecture.

pytubefix provides playlist enumeration + video metadata that this repo doesn't. Also has its own caption extraction (yt.captions) as backup Layer 2. Use together: pytubefix for discovery, youtube-transcript-api for extraction.
faster-whisper is the Layer 3 fallback when transcripts are unavailable (TranscriptsDisabled/NoTranscriptFound exceptions). Not analyzed yet.
